title: Axion Permissions
description: Declares the native capabilities an Axion app requires. Every key defaults to false/denied when omitted. Unrecognised keys are rejected at startup to catch typos early.

fs.description: Filesystem access. Omit this key entirely to deny all fs.* RPC calls. Include it with granular flags to enable specific access levels.

appData.description: Read/write access to the app's AppData sandbox: AppData/Local/Axion/<AppName>/. Required by fs.read, fs.write, and fs.delete.

userSelected.description: Access to directories explicitly chosen by the user via a system directory picker (fs.pickDirectory). Grants access only to the directory the user selected.

absolutePath.description: Read/write access to arbitrary absolute paths on the filesystem. This is the most powerful filesystem permission — only grant if strictly required.

storage.description: Persistent key-value storage: storage.get, storage.set, storage.remove. Data is scoped to the app's AppData sandbox.

notifications.description: Permission to show system desktop notifications via notifications.show.

system.description: Read-only access to system information: OS name, platform, version. Exposed via system.info, system.platform, system.version.

window.description: Window management: minimize, maximize, close, and set the window title. Exposed via window.minimize, window.maximize, window.close, window.setTitle.
